public_links.title: Wikipedia: Constructed Wetland Mechanical & Biological Engineering Principles
Wikipedia: Greywater Properties, Treatment, and Recycling Systems
Appropedia: Open-Source Constructed Wetlands for Greywater Treatment Manuals

technical_documents.description: An off-grid, ecologically engineered decentralized wastewater recycling infrastructure blueprint. Utilizing a multi-stage horizontal subsurface flow (HSSF) constructed wetland matrix, this system combines primary mechanical sedimentation, anaerobic bio-filtration, and active aerobic rhizosphere root-zone polishing to transmute raw domestic greywater into pathogen-safe, nutrient-rich effluent optimized for subsurface agricultural irrigation.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Domestic greywater accounts for over 60% of total household wastewater volume, yet it is typically discharged into unlined pit latrines or open drainage ditches. This practice causes severe shallow groundwater contamination via nitrate overloading and waterborne pathogen propagation, while simultaneously wasting upwards of 120 litres of highly reusable, localized irrigation water daily per household in climate-stressed agrarian regions.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: The design deploys an optimized three-cell horizontal subsurface flow constructed wetland lined with a heavy-duty 1.14mm EPDM impermeable membrane, sloped at a precise 1% hydraulic gradient. Raw greywater undergoes passive inline grease-trapping before entering Cell 1 for physical sedimentation. It then transitions to Cell 2 and Cell 3, which are packed with a graded capillary matrix of sieved gravel ($10	ext{--}40	ext{mm}$) and actively rooted with high-transpiration Phragmites australis reed plugs. Pathogen neutralization and surfactant breakdown are accomplished via mechanical depth straining, anaerobic microbial fixed-film filtration, and aerobic microbial predation within the dense rhizosphere root zones, purifying effluent to strictly meet WHO unrestricted subsurface irrigation safety baselines using zero grid power or chemical inputs.

bill_of_materials.name: Heavy-Duty Impermeable EPDM Geomembrane Pond Liner (1.14mm Thickness, 3m x 4m)
Sieved and Graded Silica Capillary Support Gravel Matrix (10-40mm Mix, 200 kg)
High-Transpiration Phragmites Australis Hydrophytic Reed Plugs (x20 Stock)
Rigid PVC Airway Baffles, Perforated Diffuser Manifolds, & Transfer Pipes Assembly
Dual-Chamber Mechanical First-Stage Solid Sedimentation & Baffle Grease Trap

specifications.filtration_capacity: Three-stage multi-cell horizontal subsurface flow matrix

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Excavate a 3m x 1.2m trench, 60cm deep with a 1% slope; line with EPDM pond liner.
Step 2: Divide into three cells with PVC baffles: Cell 1 (sedimentation, 40cm), Cell 2 (anaerobic, 80cm), Cell 3 (aerobic, 60cm).
Step 3: Layer graded gravel — coarse at bottom (40mm), medium (20mm), fine (10mm) at top in each cell.
Step 4: Plant 6–8 Phragmites reed plugs per square metre in Cells 2 and 3.
Step 5: Connect kitchen/bathroom drain to grease trap, then to Cell 1 inlet; after 4 weeks of plant establishment, test effluent with a basic water quality kit.

comments.text: Our garden irrigation is now 100% from treated greywater. Reeds grew 1.5m in 3 months.
Does this handle laundry detergent? Concerned about surfactants killing the reeds.
